title: Provider-lock cleanup — cloudy src/ remaining 3 hits (follow-up to T-009)
description: Follow-up carve-out from T-009. The T-009 scope restricted to docs (NEVER touch src/ per safety guard), but full-repo gate AC #2 requires check-provider-lock.sh . to exit 0. Three src/ hits remain.

context.text: Remaining hits after T-009 shipped: src/reviewer.ts:248 JSDoc 'using Claude.', src/dashboard/client/DaemonApp.tsx:5206 user-facing error string 'use Claude Code directly', src/dashboard/client/DaemonApp.tsx:5969 GPT-4 trivia Easter egg. Each needs either an inline provider-lock:allow hatch (when legitimately arch-comparison) or a rewrite to engine-agnostic wording (when prescriptive). User-facing error text at :5206 should be rewritten, not hatched.

acceptanceCriteria: src/reviewer.ts:248 — review JSDoc context; if describing one engine family's reviewing style, hatch as arch-comparison; if prescriptive, rewrite
src/dashboard/client/DaemonApp.tsx:5206 — user-facing error text REWRITTEN to engine-agnostic language (e.g. 'Use a configured coding-agent CLI directly')
src/dashboard/client/DaemonApp.tsx:5969 — Easter egg: if it's trivia/fun content referencing a model name, hatch as arch-comparison; if it prescribes routing, rewrite
`bash scripts/check-provider-lock.sh .` exits 0 (AC #2 from T-009 now fully met)
Full typecheck + test suite green after edits
Commit + push on main with pre-commit hook active
quality_gates: User-facing error strings rewritten (not hatched) — Luke could see these
Code-reviewer subagent clean on the diff
